creator: Julie Bort
title: (No AI) Databricks bought two startups to underpin its new AI security product
dc:creator: Julie Bort
content: With an overflowing war chest from its recent $5 billion raise, Databricks is buying startups and looking for more. It acquired Antimatter and SiftD.ai.
contentSnippet: With an overflowing war chest from its recent $5 billion raise, Databricks is buying startups and looking for more. It acquired Antimatter and SiftD.ai.

creator: Rebecca Szkutak
title: (No AI) Arm is releasing the first in-house chip in its 35-year history
dc:creator: Rebecca Szkutak
content: Arm is producing its own CPU for the first time. It developed the CPU with Meta, which is also the chip's first customer.
contentSnippet: Arm is producing its own CPU for the first time. It developed the CPU with Meta, which is also the chip's first customer.

title: (No AI) ChatGPT and Gemini are fighting to be the AI bot that sells you stuff
author: Emma Roth
content:
![Photo collage of a computer with the ChatGPT logo on the screen.](https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/chorus/uploads/chorus_asset/file/25362057/STK_414_AI_CHATBOT_R2_CVirginia_B.jpg?quality=90&strip=all&crop=0,0,100,100)

The AI-powered shopping rivalry is heating up as Google and OpenAI launch new features to help you buy things while interacting with their chatbots. Now, Google is teaming up with [Gap Inc to allow its Gemini AI assistant](https://www.gapinc.com/en-us/articles/2026/03/gap-inc-elevates-online-shopping-with-ai-powered-f) to purchase clothes on your behalf from any of its stores, which include Gap, Old Navy, Banana Republic, and Athleta, while OpenAI [launched an updated shopping interface in ChatGPT](https://openai.com/index/powering-product-discovery-in-chatgpt/).

With Google's new partnership, you may see products from one of Gap's brands if you're asking Gemini for shopping suggestions, along with an option to purchase the item without leaving the chatbot. Similar to Walmart and Target, which also let u …

[Read the full story at The Verge.](https://www.theverge.com/ai-artificial-intelligence/899677/openai-google-gemini-ai-shopping-features)
contentSnippet:
The AI-powered shopping rivalry is heating up as Google and OpenAI launch new features to help you buy things while interacting with their chatbots. Now, Google is teaming up with Gap Inc to allow its Gemini AI assistant to purchase clothes on your behalf from any of its stores, which include Gap, Old Navy, Banana Republic, and Athleta, while OpenAI launched an updated shopping interface in ChatGPT.
With Google's new partnership, you may see products from one of Gap's brands if you're asking Gemini for shopping suggestions, along with an option to purchase the item without leaving the chatbot. Similar to Walmart and Target, which also let u …
Read the full story at The Verge.

Now, Google is teaming up with Gap Inc to allow its Gemini AI assistant to purchase clothes on your behalf from any of its stores, which include Gap, Old Navy, Banana […]

title: (No AI) Anthropic’s Claude Code and Cowork can control your computer
author: Jess Weatherbed
content:
![A screenshot of the warning message that appears in Claude before allowing the AI chatbot to control your computer.](https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2026/03/Anthropic-Claude-Code-Cowork-computer-control.jpg?quality=90&strip=all&crop=0,0,100,100)

Claude will ask you for permission to autonomously perform taks on your computer. | Image by Anthropic

Anthropic has updated Claude to perform tasks in its Code and Cowork AI tools autonomously by using your computer for you. The new feature can be used to automatically open files, use web browsers and apps, and run dev tools "with no setup required," even when you're away from your computer, according to [Anthropic's announcement](https://claude.com/blog/dispatch-and-computer-use).

These new capabilities are available as a research preview for Claude Pro and Max subscribers, and computer usage is limited to macOS "for now," according to Anthropic. The feature [builds on autonomous capabilities](https://www.theverge.com/2024/10/22/24276822/anthopic-claude-3-5-sonnet-computer-use-ai) that were introduced on Claude's 3.5 Sonnet AI model in 2024, but now brings those to [the chatbot's C …](https://www.theverge.com/report/874308/anthropic-claude-code-opus-hype-moment)

[Read the full story at The Verge.](https://www.theverge.com/ai-artificial-intelligence/899430/anthropic-claude-code-cowork-ai-control-computer)

These new capabilities are available as a research preview for Claude Pro and Max subscribers, and computer usage is limited to macOS "for now," according to Anthropic. The feature builds on autonomous capabilities that were introduced on Claude's 3.5 Sonnet AI model in 2024, but now brings those to the chatbot's C …
Read the full story at The Verge.

title: (No AI) Helping developers build safer AI experiences for teens
content: OpenAI releases prompt-based teen safety policies for developers using gpt-oss-safeguard, helping moderate age-specific risks in AI systems.
contentSnippet: OpenAI releases prompt-based teen safety policies for developers using gpt-oss-safeguard, helping moderate age-specific risks in AI systems.

title: (No AI) Update on the OpenAI Foundation
content: The OpenAI Foundation announces plans to invest at least $1 billion in curing diseases, economic opportunity, AI resilience, and community programs.
contentSnippet: The OpenAI Foundation announces plans to invest at least $1 billion in curing diseases, economic opportunity, AI resilience, and community programs.

title: (No AI) Measuring progress toward AGI: A cognitive framework
content: We’re introducing a framework to measure progress toward AGI, and launching a Kaggle hackathon to build the relevant evaluations.
contentSnippet: We’re introducing a framework to measure progress toward AGI, and launching a Kaggle hackathon to build the relevant evaluations.

title: (No AI) From games to biology and beyond: 10 years of AlphaGo’s impact
content: Ten years since AlphaGo, we explore how it is catalyzing scientific discovery and paving a path to AGI.
contentSnippet: Ten years since AlphaGo, we explore how it is catalyzing scientific discovery and paving a path to AGI.

creator: Lauren Goode
title: (No AI) Arm Is Now Making Its Own Chips
dc:creator: Lauren Goode
content: The chip design firm says Meta, OpenAI, Cerebras, and Cloudflare are among the first customers of its new artificial intelligence hardware.
contentSnippet: The chip design firm says Meta, OpenAI, Cerebras, and Cloudflare are among the first customers of its new artificial intelligence hardware.

creator: Katie Drummond
title: (No AI) Chris Hayes Has Some Advice for Keeping Up With the News
dc:creator: Katie Drummond
content: The host of MS Now’s All In, knows how hard it is to stay current. But he also knows where you should focus your attention—and it starts with a sober view of AI.
contentSnippet: The host of MS Now’s All In, knows how hard it is to stay current. But he also knows where you should focus your attention—and it starts with a sober view of AI.
